title: Novel cytochrome p450 fusion protein
abstract: This disclosure relates to the isolation of a nucleic acid molecule[s] that encode a novel cytochrome P450 and an oxidoreductase from a Papaver somniferum [P. somniferum] cultivar, transgenic cells transformed with said nucleic acid molecule and sequence variants thereof; and including methods for the production of intermediates in the production of morphinans.
claims:
Claims Claims An isolated polypeptide wherein said polypeptide comprises: i) a domain comprising an amino acid sequence comprising a cytochrome P450 activity wherein said activity catalyses the conversion of (S)-reticuline to 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline; and ii) a domain comprising an amino acid sequence comprising an oxidoreductase activity wherein said activity catalyses the conversion of 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline to (/^-reticuline. 2. The polypeptide according to claim 1 wherein said polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of: i) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 ; or ii) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 2 ;or iii) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 3; or iv) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 1 ; or v) a modified polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence that is at least 50% identical to the full length amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 , 2, 3 or 11 , wherein said sequence is modified by addition, deletion or substitution of one or more amino acid sequences and wherein said polypeptide has retained or enhanced enzyme activity when compared to the polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 , 2, 3 or 1 1 ; and/or vi) a polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence KPCVQSAASERD set forth in SEQ ID NO: 7. 3. The polypeptide according to claim 1 or 2 comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 2, or an amino acid sequence that is at least 59% identical to SEQ ID NO: 2, wherein the polypeptide is an in-frame translational fusion with an oxidoreductase polypeptide. 4. The polypeptide according to claim 1 or 2 comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 3, or an amino acid sequence that is at least 75% identical to SEQ ID NO: 3, wherein the polypeptide is an in-frame translational fusion with an cytochrome P450 polypeptide. An isolated polypeptide wherein said polypeptide is selected from the group sting of: i) a domain comprising an amino acid sequence comprising a cytochrome P450 activity wherein said activity catalyses the conversion of (S)-reticuline to 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline; or ii) a modified polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence that is at least 59% identical to the full length amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 2, wherein said sequence is modified by addition, deletion or substitution of one or more amino acid sequences and wherein said polypeptide has retained or enhanced enzyme activity when compared to the polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 2. 6. An isolated polypeptide wherein said polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of: i) a domain comprising an amino acid sequence comprising an oxidoreductase activity wherein said activity catalyses the conversion of 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline to (/^-reticuline; or ii) a modified polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence that is at least 75% identical to the full length amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 3, wherein said sequence is modified by addition, deletion or substitution of one or more amino acid sequences and wherein said polypeptide has retained or enhanced enzyme activity when compared to the polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 3. 7. An isolated polypeptide wherein said polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of: i) a domain comprising an amino acid sequence comprising an oxidoreductase activity wherein said activity catalyses the conversion of 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline to (/^-reticuline; or ii) a modified polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence that is at least 75% identical to the full length amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 1 , wherein said sequence is modified by addition, deletion or substitution of one or more amino acid sequences and wherein said polypeptide has retained or enhanced enzyme activity when compared to the polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 11. 8. An isolated nucleic acid molecule that encodes a polypeptide according to any one of claims 1 to 7. 9. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 8 is selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i); iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9 and which encodes a polypeptide that has cytochrome P450 and/or oxidoreductase activity as herein disclosed. 10. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 8 or 9 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises a nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 4 wherein said nucleotide sequence comprises an open reading frame between codons 1 to 2706. 1 1. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 10 wherein said open reading frame comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 29. 12. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 8 or 9 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises a nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 6 wherein said nucleotide sequence comprises an open reading frame between codons 1 to 966. 13. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 12 wherein said open reading frame comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 50. 14. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 8 or 9 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises a nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 9. 15. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 14 wherein said open reading frame comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10. 16. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 8 or 9 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists of a nucleotide sequence as represented in SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9. 17. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 8 or 9 wherein said nucleic acid molecule is selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10, 29, 31 , 32 33 or 50; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i); iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10, 29, 31 , 32, 33 or 50, and which encodes a polypeptide that has cytochrome P450 and/or oxidoreductase activity as herein disclosed. 18. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 17 wherein said isolated nucleic acid molecule is at least 70% identical over the full nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10, 29, 31 , 32, 33 or 50. 19. The isolated nucleic acid molecule according to claim 17 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists of a nucleotide sequence as represented in SEQ ID NO: 10, 29, 31 , 32, 33 or 50. 20. A vector comprising a nucleic acid molecule encoding a polypeptide according to any one of claims 1 to 7 wherein said nucleic acid molecule is operably linked to a nucleic acid molecule comprising a promoter sequence. 21. An isolated transgenic cell transformed or transfected with a nucleic acid molecule or vector according to any one of claims 8 to 20. 22. The cell according to claim 21 wherein said cell is a plant cell. 23. The cell according to claim 21 wherein said cell is a microbial cell. 24. The cell according to claim 23 wherein said microbial cell is a bacterial cell. 25. The cell according to claim 23 wherein said microbial cell is a yeast cell. 26. The cell according to any one of claims 21 to 25 wherein said nucleic acid molecule or vector comprises a nucleotide sequence encoding a cytochrome P450 domain selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 5 or 32; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i); iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 5 or 32 and which encodes a polypeptide that has cytochrome P450 activity. 27. The cell according to any one of claims 21 to 25 wherein said nucleic acid molecule or vector comprises a nucleotide sequence encoding an oxidoreductase domain selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 6, 10, 33 or 50; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i); iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 6, 10 or 33 or 50 and which encodes a polypeptide that has oxidoreductase activity. 28. The cell according to any one of claims 23 to 25 wherein said microbial cell expresses a cytochrome P450 reductase. 29. The cell according to claim 28 wherein said microbial cell expresses an NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase. 30. The cell according to claim 28 or 29 wherein said microbial cell is transformed with a nucleic acid molecule or vector comprising said cytochrome P450 reductase or NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase. 31. The cell according to any one of claims 28 to 30 wherein said reductase is a plant NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase. 32. The cell according to any one of claims 28 to 31 wherein said cytochrome P450 reductase is encoded by a nucleotide sequence selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 30; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i); iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 30 and which encodes a polypeptide that has NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase activity. 33. The cell according to claim 32 wherein said nucleotide sequence is at least 70% identical to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 30 over the full nucleotide sequence. 34. The cell according to any one of claims 28 to 31 wherein said cytochrome P450 reductase is encoded by a nucleotide sequence selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 51 ; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i); iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 51 and which encodes a polypeptide that has NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase activity. 35. The cell according to claim 34 wherein said nucleotide sequence is at least 70% identical to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 51 over the full nucleotide sequence. 36. A transgenic plant comprising a plant cell according to claim 22. 37. The plant according to claim 36 wherein said plant is from the genus Papaver; preferably Papaver somniferum, P. setigerum, P. bracteatum, P. orientate, P. pseudo- orientale, P. lasiothrix, P. cylindricum, P. fugax, P. triniifolium. 38. A process for the production of a polypeptide comprising: i) providing a cell that expresses a polypeptide selected from the group consisting of: a) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 ; or b) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 2; or c) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 3; or d) a polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 1 ; or e) a modified polypeptide comprising an amino acid sequence that is at least 50% identical to the full length amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 , 2, 3 or 11 , wherein said sequence is modified by addition, deletion or substitution of one or more amino acid sequences when compared to the polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 1 , 2, 3 or 11 ; and/or f) a polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence KPCVQSAASERD as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 7; ii) culturing said cell under cell culture conditions conducive to the expression of said polypeptide; and optionally iii) extracting said polypeptide from the cell or cell culture medium. 39. The process according to claim 38 wherein said cell is a transgenic cell according to any one of claims 21 to 31. 40. The process according to claim 38 or 39 wherein said cell is a yeast cell. 41. A nucleic acid molecule comprising a transcription cassette wherein said cassette includes a nucleotide sequence designed with reference to SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9 and is adapted for expression by provision of at least one promoter operably linked to said 

 nucleotide sequence such that both sense and antisense molecules are transcribed from said cassette. 42. The nucleic acid molecule according to claim 41 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises a transcription cassette wherein said cassette includes a nucleotide sequence designed with reference to SEQ ID NO: 29. 43. The nucleic acid molecule according to claim 41 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises a transcription cassette wherein said cassette includes a nucleotide sequence designed with reference to SEQ ID NO: 50. 44. The nucleic acid molecule according to claim 41 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises a transcription cassette wherein said cassette includes a nucleotide sequence designed with reference to SEQ ID NO: 10. 45. The nucleic acid molecule according to any one of claims 41 to 44 wherein said nucleic acid molecule is part of a vector adapted for expression in a plant cell. 46. A plant cell transfected with a nucleic acid molecule or vector according to any one of claims 41 to 45 wherein said cell has reduced expression of the nucleic acid molecule according to any one of claims 8 to 19. 47. A process for the transformation of (S)-reticuline to (/^-reticuline comprising: i) providing a transgenic plant cell according to claim 22; ii) cultivating said plant cell to produce a transgenic plant; and optionally iii) harvesting said transgenic plant, or part thereof. 48. The process according to claim 47 wherein the transformation of (S)-reticuline to (/^-reticuline is via 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline. 49. The process according to claim 47 or 48 wherein said plant cell is transformed with a nucleic acid molecule encoding a cytochrome P450 reductase. 50. The process according to claim 49 wherein said plant cell is transformed with a nucleic acid molecule encoding an NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase. 51. The process according to any one of claims 47 to 50 wherein said harvested plant material is dried and (/^-reticuline is extracted. 52. A process for the transformation of (S)-reticuline to (/^-reticuline comprising: i) providing a transgenic microbial cell according to any one of claims 23 to 35 that expresses the nucleic acid molecule according to any one of claims 8 to 19 in culture with at least (S)-reticuline; ii) cultivating the microbial cell under conditions that transform (S)- reticuline; and optionally iii) isolating (/^-reticuline from the microbial cell or cell culture. 53. The process according to claim 52 wherein the transformation of (S)-reticuline to (/^-reticuline is via 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline. 54. The process according to claim 52 or 53 wherein said microbial cell is transformed with a nucleic acid molecule encoding a cytochrome P450 reductase. 55. The process according to claim 54 wherein said microbial cell is transformed with a nucleic acid molecule encoding an NADH or NADPH dependent cytochrome P450 reductase. 56. A process for the transformation of 1 , 2-dehydroreticuline to (/^-reticuline comprising: i) providing a transgenic microbial cell any one of claims 23 to 35 that expresses the nucleic acid molecule according any one of claims 8 to 19 in culture with at least 1 ,2-dehydroreticuline; ii) cultivating the microbial cell under conditions that transform 1 ,2- dehydroreticuline; and optionally iii) isolating (/^-reticuline from the microbial cell or cell culture. 57. The process according to any one of claims 52 to 56 wherein said microbial cell is a bacterial cell or fungal/yeast cell. 58. The use of a polypeptide or nucleic acid molecule according to any one of claims 1 to 19 in engineering one or more metabolic pathways in a host microbial cell. 59. The use of a gene encoded by a nucleic acid molecule comprising the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9, or a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9 and encodes a polypeptide according to the invention as a means to identify a locus wherein said locus is associated with altered expression or activity of a cytochrome P450 or oxidoreductase. 60. The use according to claim 59 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 29. 61. The use according to claim 59 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10. 62. The use according to claim 59 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 50. 63. A method to produce a Papaver plant that has altered expression of a polypeptide according to the invention comprising the steps of: i) mutagenesis of wild-type seed from a Papaver plant that does express said polypeptide; ii) cultivation of the seed in i) to produce first and subsequent generations of plants; iii) obtaining seed from the first generation plant and subsequent generations of plants; iv) determining if the seed from said first and subsequent generations of plants has altered nucleotide sequence and/or altered expression of said polypeptide; v) obtaining a sample and analysing the nucleic acid sequence of a nucleic acid molecule selected from the group consisting of: i. a nucleic acid molecule comprising a nucleotide sequence as represented in SEQ ID NO: 4, 5, 6 or 9; ii. a nucleic acid molecule that hybridises to a complementary strand of said nucleic acid molecule in a) under stringent hybridisation conditions and that encodes a polypeptide with cytochrome P450 and/or oxidoreductase polypeptide activity; and optionally 

 vi) comparing the nucleotide sequence of the nucleic acid molecule in said sample to a nucleotide sequence of a nucleic acid molecule of the original wild-type plant. 64. The method according to any one of claims 63 wherein said nucleic acid molecule is analysed by a method comprising the steps of: i) extracting nucleic acid from said mutated plants; ii) amplification of a part of said nucleic acid molecule by a polymerase chain reaction; iii) forming a preparation comprising the amplified nucleic acid and nucleic acid extracted from wild-type seed to form heteroduplex nucleic acid; iv) incubating said preparation with a single stranded nuclease that cuts at a region of heteroduplex nucleic acid to identify the mismatch in said heteroduplex; and v) determining the site of the mismatch in said nucleic acid heteroduplex. 65. The method according to claim 63 or 64 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 29. 66. The method according to claim 63 or 64 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10. 67. The method according to 63 or 64 wherein said nucleic acid molecule comprises the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 50. 68. The method according to any one of claims 63 to 67 wherein said Papaver plant is Papaver somniferum, P. setigerum, P. bracteatum, P. orientate, P. pseudo-orientale, P. lasiothrix, P. cylindricum, P. fugax, P. triniifolium. 69. A plant obtained by the method according to any one of claims 63 to 68. 70. A plant wherein said plant comprises a viral vector that includes all or part of a gene comprising a nucleic acid molecule according to any one of claims 8 to 19. 71. A viral vector comprising all or part of a nucleic acid molecule according to any one of 8 to 19. 72. The use of a viral vector according to claim 71 in viral induced gene silencing in a plant. 73. An isolated nucleic acid molecule selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 30; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i) above; iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 30 which encodes a polypeptide that has cytochrome P450 reductase activity. 74. An isolated nucleic acid molecule selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i) above; iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10 which encodes a polypeptide that has oxidoreductase activity 75. An isolated nucleic acid molecule selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 50; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i) above; iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 50 which encodes a polypeptide that has oxidoreductase activity 76. An isolated nucleic acid molecule selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 29; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i) above; 

 iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 29 which encodes a polypeptide that has cytochrome P450 and/or oxidoreductase activity 77. An isolated nucleic acid molecule selected from the group consisting of: i) a nucleotide sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 31 , 32 or 33; ii) a nucleotide sequence wherein said sequence is degenerate as a result of the genetic code to the nucleotide sequence defined in (i) above; iii) a nucleic acid molecule the complementary strand of which hybridizes under stringent hybridization conditions to the nucleotide sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 31 , 32 or 33 which encodes a polypeptide that has cytochrome P450 and/or oxidoreductase activity.
